---
title: "LPSspleen_DCsubset_GSEA analysis"
date: "2024-11-15"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(enrichplot)
library(BioNet)
library(stringr)
library(pcaMethods)
library(DESeq2)
library(ggpubr)
library(org.Mm.eg.db)
library(tibble)
```

### GO:BP

```{r GSEA enrichment using GO:BP, echo = TRUE}
library(clusterProfiler) 
library(org.Mm.eg.db)    
library(dplyr)           

# Read and preprocess each group
groups <- list(
  norm_lpsin = read.csv("path-to-your-file/Norm_vs_LPSin.csv"),    
  norm_lpsout = read.csv("path-to-your-file/Norm_vs_LPSin/Norm_vs_LPSout.csv"),  
  lpsin_lpsout = read.csv("path-to-your-file/Norm_vs_LPSin/LPSin_vs_LPSout.csv") 
)

# Initialize list to store results
gsea_results <- list()

# Process each group
for (group_name in names(groups)) {
  # Extract group data
  group_data <- groups[[group_name]]
  
  # Extract log fold-change values and gene names
  ranks <- group_data$logFC
  names(ranks) <- group_data$Genes
  
  # Convert Gene Symbols to ENTREZ IDs
  gene_conversion <- bitr(
    names(ranks),
    fromType = "SYMBOL",
    toType = "ENTREZID",
    OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db
  )
  
  # Filter and match ranks with converted IDs
  gene_scores_entrez <- ranks[gene_conversion$SYMBOL]
  names(gene_scores_entrez) <- gene_conversion$ENTREZID
  
  # Remove NA values to avoid errors during plotting or GSEA
  gene_scores_entrez <- na.omit(gene_scores_entrez)
  
  # Barplot of gene scores
  barplot(
    sort(gene_scores_entrez, decreasing = TRUE),
    main = paste("Gene Scores (ENTREZ IDs) for", group_name),
    xlab = "Genes (ENTREZ IDs)",
    ylab = "Log Fold Change",
    las = 2,  # Rotate axis labels
    col = "blue"
  )
  
 # Perform GSEA using KEGG
geneList <- sort(gene_scores_entrez, decreasing = TRUE)

# Perform GSEA using GO Biological Process (BP)
gsea_result <- gseGO(
  geneList = geneList,             # Ranked list of genes
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,            # Mouse organism database
  ont = "BP",                      # Ontology: Biological Process
  minGSSize = 3,                   # Minimum size of gene sets
  maxGSSize = 800,                 # Maximum size of gene sets
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05,             # p-value cutoff
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",            # Benjamini-Hochberg correction
  keyType = "ENTREZID"             # ID type (ENTREZ IDs)
)
  # Store GSEA results for the group
  gsea_results[[group_name]] <- gsea_result
  
  # Print summary of GSEA results for this group
  print(paste("GSEA results for", group_name))
  print(head(gsea_result))
}
 
```

```{r plot bubble plot, echo=TRUE}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)

# Iterate through each group's GSEA results
for (group_name in names(gsea_results)) {
  # Convert GSEA result to a data frame
  gsea_results_df <- as.data.frame(gsea_results[[group_name]])
  
  # Remove "Mus musculus (house mouse)" from the description
  gsea_results_df$Description <- gsub("- .*", "", gsea_results_df$Description)
  
  # Filter results to include only significant pathways (adjust as needed)
  gsea_results_df_filtered <- gsea_results_df[gsea_results_df$p.adjust < 0.05, ]
  
  # Save filtered GSEA results as a .csv file
  # Save to the download folder
  if (nrow(gsea_results_df_filtered) > 0) {
    write.csv(
      gsea_results_df_filtered,
      file = paste0("GSEA_Results_", group_name, ".csv"),
      row.names = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    message(paste("No significant pathways to save for group:", group_name))
  }
  
  # Select the top 25 pathways by adjusted p-value
  gsea_results_df_top25 <- gsea_results_df_filtered[order(gsea_results_df_filtered$p.adjust), ][1:25, ]
  
  # Check if there are enough pathways to plot
  if (nrow(gsea_results_df_top25) > 0) {
    # Create a bubble plot
    bubble_plot <- ggplot(gsea_results_df_top25, aes(x = NES, 
                                                     y = reorder(Description, NES), 
                                                     size = setSize, 
                                                     color = p.adjust)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
      scale_color_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue", name = "Adjusted P-Value") +
      labs(title = paste("Enriched GO Pathways -", group_name),
           x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
           y = "Pathway Description",
           size = "gene size") +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
            axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 1)) 
    
        # Save the plot to a PDF file
        # It is saved to the download folder 
    ggsave(filename = paste0("Enriched_GO_Pathways_", group_name, ".pdf"),
           plot = bubble_plot,
           width = 10, height = 8, device = "pdf")
    
    # Display the plot
    print(bubble_plot)
  } else {
    message(paste("No significant pathways for group:", group_name))
  }
}
```

```{r plot bubble plot specified pathway norm_lpsin, echo=TRUE}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)

# Specify the group name to process
group_name <- "norm_lpsin"

# Convert GSEA result to a data frame
gsea_results_df <- as.data.frame(gsea_results[[group_name]])
  
# Remove "Mus musculus (house mouse)" from the description
gsea_results_df$Description <- gsub("- .*", "", gsea_results_df$Description)
  
# Filter results to include only significant pathways (adjust as needed)
gsea_results_df_filtered <- gsea_results_df[gsea_results_df$p.adjust < 0.05, ]
  
# List of specified pathways
specified_pathways <- c("cellular response to cytokine stimulus", "defense response", "intermediate filament-based process", "intermediate filament cytoskeleton organization", "response to interferon-alpha", "response to bacterium", "immune response", "defense response to bacterium", "cytosolic pattern recognition receptor signaling pathway", "intracellular receptor signaling pathway", "regulation of response to biotic stimulus", "positive regulation of innate immune response", "regulation of innate immune response", "cytokine-mediated signaling pathway", "response to type II interferon", "response to lipopolysaccharide", "cytoskeleton organization", "supramolecular fiber organization")
  
# Filter the data frame for the specified pathways
gsea_results_df_specified <- gsea_results_df_filtered %>% dplyr::filter(Description %in% specified_pathways)
  
  # Check if there are enough pathways to plot
  if (nrow(gsea_results_df_specified) > 0) {
    # Create a bubble plot
    bubble_plot <- ggplot(gsea_results_df_specified, aes(x = NES, 
                                                     y = reorder(Description, NES), 
                                                     size = setSize, 
                                                     color = p.adjust)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
      scale_color_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue", name = "Adjusted P-Value") +
      labs(title = paste("Inside vs. Norm"),
           x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
           y = "Pathway Description",
           size = "gene size") +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 1),
            axis.title = element_text(size=14))
    
        # Save the plot to a PDF file
        # It is saved to the download folder 
    #ggsave(filename = paste0("Enriched_GO_Pathways_", group_name, ".pdf"),
           #plot = bubble_plot,
           #width = 10, height = 8, device = "pdf")
    
    # Display the plot
    print(bubble_plot)
  } else {
    message(paste("No significant pathways for group:", group_name))
  }

```

```{r plot bubble plot specified pathway norm_lpsout, echo=TRUE}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)

# Specify the group name to process
group_name <- "norm_lpsout"

# Convert GSEA result to a data frame
gsea_results_df <- as.data.frame(gsea_results[[group_name]])
  
# Remove "Mus musculus (house mouse)" from the description
gsea_results_df$Description <- gsub("- .*", "", gsea_results_df$Description)
  
# Filter results to include only significant pathways (adjust as needed)
gsea_results_df_filtered <- gsea_results_df[gsea_results_df$p.adjust < 0.05, ]
  
# List of specified pathways
specified_pathways <- c("cellular response to interferon-beta","defense response to bacterium","immune response", "response to cytokine","response to lipopolysaccharide","response to molecule of bacterial origin","intracellular receptor signaling pathway","cellular response to cytokine stimulus","organelle localization","positive regulation of response to external stimulus","regulation of inflammasome-mediated signaling pathway","activation of innate immune response","leukocyte migration involved in inflammatory response","cytosolic pattern recognition receptor signaling pathway","canonical inflammasome complex assembly","defense response to Gram-negative bacterium","antimicrobial humoral response","cellular response to type II interferon","establishment of organelle localization")
  
# Filter the data frame for the specified pathways
gsea_results_df_specified <- gsea_results_df_filtered %>% dplyr::filter(Description %in% specified_pathways)
  
  # Check if there are enough pathways to plot
  if (nrow(gsea_results_df_specified) > 0) {
    # Create a bubble plot
    bubble_plot <- ggplot(gsea_results_df_specified, aes(x = NES, 
                                                     y = reorder(Description, NES), 
                                                     size = setSize, 
                                                     color = p.adjust)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
      scale_color_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue", name = "Adjusted P-Value") +
      labs(title = paste("Outside vs. Norm"),
           x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
           y = "Pathway Description",
           size = "gene size") +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 1),
            axis.title = element_text(size=14))
    
        # Save the plot to a PDF file
        # It is saved to the download folder 
    #ggsave(filename = paste0("Enriched_GO_Pathways_", group_name, ".pdf"),
           #plot = bubble_plot,
           #width = 10, height = 8, device = "pdf")
    
    # Display the plot
    print(bubble_plot)
  } else {
    message(paste("No significant pathways for group:", group_name))
  }

```
